insight: The hydration breaks in the 2026 FIFA World Cup seem to have a positive impact on Morocco's performance, as they gained momentum in both of their breaks, with a mean increase of 0.250 points per minute. This suggests that the team is able to regroup and refocus during these breaks, coming back onto the field with renewed energy and intensity. Notably, in the instances where Morocco was not pressing before the break, they were still able to gain momentum afterwards, maintaining an average increase of 0.250 points per minute, indicating that the breaks provide a valuable opportunity for the team to adjust their strategy and come back stronger. Overall, the data implies that Morocco is able to effectively utilize the hydration breaks to their advantage, using the time to recharge and gain an edge over their opponents.
